name: Agreena Carbon Farming and Soil Carbon Credit API
what_it_does: Agreena carbon farming and soil carbon credit REST API for European agricultural companies, food producers, and supply chain managers to access soil carbon measurement data, issue verified carbon certificates (aAMUs), integrate carbon farming program enrollment, and connect with Agreena's regenerative agriculture platform serving 1M+ hectares across Europe — enabling digital measurement, reporting, and verification (dMRV) of soil carbon sequestration, regenerative agriculture certification, and supply chain carbon transparency through Agreena's scientifically validated soil carbon methodology. Enables AI agents to manage farm enrollment for European carbon farming program automation, handle soil carbon measurement for carbon credit verification automation, access carbon certificate for aAMU issuance and transfer automation, retrieve field data for regenerative practice tracking automation, manage payment for carbon credit disbursement to farmers automation, handle supply chain for food producer carbon footprint reporting automation, access MRV data for carbon credit verification reporting automation, retrieve farmer report for regenerative agriculture performance automation, manage contract for carbon farming agreement automation, and integrate Agreena with European food supply chain, sustainability reporting, and ESG platforms for soil carbon management automation.
use_cases: Enrolling European farms in Agreena carbon farming programs for soil carbon credit generation automation agents
Accessing soil carbon measurement data for carbon credit verification and MRV reporting automation agents
Integrating Agreena carbon certificates into food supply chain sustainability reporting for ESG automation agents
Managing carbon farming contracts and farmer payment disbursements for agricultural carbon program automation agents
not_for: Non-European markets primarily (Agreena focuses on European arable farming)
Forest carbon credits (Agreena focuses on soil/arable land carbon, not forestry REDD+)
Voluntary carbon market trading (Agreena generates certificates for supply chain, not exchange-traded carbon)
best_when: A European food producer, agricultural company, or supply chain sustainability manager wanting AI agents to enroll farms in soil carbon programs, access verified carbon measurement data, and integrate Agreena carbon certificates into ESG and supply chain reporting.
avoid_when: FARM ENROLLMENT AND VERIFICATION REQUIRED: Agreena carbon farming requires physical farm enrollment with field mapping and practice verification; automated instant carbon certificate assumption creates pending_verification state; automated must complete multi-step farm enrollment and soil carbon assessment process. EUROPEAN ARABLE LAND FOCUS: Agreena's soil carbon methodology is validated for European arable crops; automated global applicability assumption creates methodology_not_applicable for non-European or non-arable land use; automated must verify Agreena coverage for specific regions. PRACTICE CHANGE IS REQUIRED: Agreena carbon farming requires transitioning to regenerative practices (no-till, cover crops, etc.); automated existing-practice carbon credit assumption misses additionality requirement; automated farmers must implement qualifying regenerative practices. ANNUAL MEASUREMENT CYCLE: Soil carbon measurement is annual via soil sampling or remote sensing; automated real-time carbon data assumption creates measurement cycle mismatch; automated must align with Agreena's annual measurement and verification schedule.

auth.notes: Agreena uses OAuth2 for API authentication. REST API with JSON. Copenhagen, Denmark HQ. Founded 2018 by Simon Haldrup. Products: Carbon farming enrollment, soil carbon MRV, aAMU carbon certificates, supply chain carbon traceability, regenerative agriculture practice tracking. SDKs: None public. EU-aligned carbon standards. 1M+ hectares enrolled. European focus (Denmark, Germany, UK, France, Poland, etc.). Backed by investors including Hummingbird Ventures. Competes with Soil Capital and Indigo for European soil carbon.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Pilot program evaluation; production API requires enterprise partnership
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-hectare enrollment; revenue share on carbon certificates; enterprise supply chain data packages
pricing.notes: Copenhagen DK. EU carbon methodology. Per-hectare + revenue share. 1M+ hectares. European soil carbon leader.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API client; data access and certificate APIs throttled

known_agent_gotchas: PHYSICAL FARM ENROLLMENT REQUIRED: Carbon credit generation requires enrolled farms with verified field boundaries and practice change documentation; automated API-only carbon certificate assumption creates no_enrolled_farm; automated must complete physical farm enrollment before API data access

ANNUAL MEASUREMENT CYCLE: Soil carbon data updates annually via soil sampling (odd years) or remote sensing (even years); automated real-time carbon data assumption creates stale measurement response; automated must align workflows with Agreena's annual measurement schedule

PRACTICE ADDITIONALITY REQUIREMENT: Carbon credits require documented practice change (adding no-till, cover crops, etc.); automated baseline-practice carbon credit assumption creates additionality_not_met; automated must verify farmers have implemented qualifying regenerative practices

aAMU CERTIFICATE FORMAT IS SPECIFIC: Agreena issues aAMU (Agreena Agricultural Measurement Unit) certificates in specific format; automated generic carbon credit format assumption creates certificate format mismatch for supply chain integration; automated must implement aAMU certificate format in sustainability systems

ENTERPRISE PARTNERSHIP FOR API ACCESS: Agreena API for supply chain integration requires enterprise partnership agreement; automated developer self-service API access assumption creates no API endpoint; automated must establish Agreena supply chain partner agreement
agent_readiness.security_notes: European carbon farming. GDPR, CSRD, EU-aligned standards. OAuth2. EU. Farm data and carbon certificate data.

security_score_components.security_notes: European soil carbon. GDPR, CSRD. OAuth2. EU. Farm and carbon certificate data.
